description: A large forest pigeon. Dark maroon-brown overall with a scaly pattern on the neck and a red bill with pale tip. Often flushed from canopy with loud wing claps.
funFact: Listen for wing claps when birds flush from tall forest; scaly neck pattern is distinctive.
voice: Deep hooting calls in forest canopy.

description: A large dark pigeon with a crisp white cap and pale eye. Body is dark gray with iridescent green on the back. Strong flier between coastal mangroves and inland feeding areas.
funFact: White crown and pale eye stand out on dark body; often flies high between coast and forest.
fieldMarks: Pale iris on dark gray body
Iridescent green on back in good light
voice: Low hooting calls; wings audible in flight.

description: A large purple-brown pigeon with a red bill and yellowish tip. Tail is gray without white markings. Common in forest and woodland, often seen flying overhead in pairs.
funFact: Purple-brown body and red-and-yellow bill distinguish it from short-billed and pale-vented pigeons.
fieldMarks: Plain gray tail, no white band

description: A pale sandy-gray dove with a narrow black collar on the hindneck and a squared tail with white corners in flight. Larger and paler than Common Ground-Dove; spread widely in towns and farmland.
funFact: Three-note cooing song and black collar are easy field marks in villages and open country.

description: A tiny dove of open ground and bare paths. Ruddy brown with scaled appearance on the head and breast; short tail shows rufous in flight. Often flushes with whistling wing beats.
funFact: Tiny size and scaled plumage distinguish it from Ruddy Ground-Dove on open ground.
fieldMarks: Very small dove on the ground
Rufous in wings and tail in flight
voice: Soft cooing; wings whistle on flush.

description: A shy forest dove walking on the ground. Warm ruddy brown with a pale stripe below the eye and iridescent purple on the back. Usually seen alone, flushing briefly from trails.
funFact: Walks quietly on forest floor; brief views when flushed show ruddy body and pale face stripe.

description: A large gray-brown ground dove. Shows a white tail tip in flight and has a pale blue orbital ring. Common in woodland edges, gardens, and secondary growth.
funFact: White tail corners flash when it flushes; pale blue eyering visible at close range.
fieldMarks: White tail tip visible in flight
voice: Deep whooing, often repeated from cover.

description: A slender brown dove with a bold white wing patch in flight and blue eyering. Tail is squared with white corners. Common in towns, scrub, and open woodland.
funFact: Bold white wing patch in flight and whooing song in settled areas.
fieldMarks: Bold white wing patch in flight

description: A slim tan-brown dove with a long pointed tail and black spots on the wings. Common in open country and towns. Flight is swift and direct with whistling wing beats.
funFact: Long pointed tail and whistling wings on flush; mournful cooing song.

description: A slender crested cuckoo of open country. Buff below with bold black streaking; long tail and shaggy crest. Often sits conspicuously on fence posts and wires.
funFact: Crested profile and streaked underparts; listen for ascending whistled song from open scrub.
voice: Ascending whistled series, often from exposed perch.

description: A long-tailed brown cuckoo with a yellow lower mandible and bold white tail spots from below. Secretive in foliage but often detected by its knocking call in migration and breeding season.
funFact: White tail spots from below and yellow bill base; listen for slow knocking song.
fieldMarks: Large white tail spots from below

description: A buff-breasted cuckoo with a black mask, yellow lower mandible, and plain buff undertail. Favors coastal thickets and mangroves but occurs inland in scrub. More often heard than seen.
funFact: Black mask and buff breast; guttural gwa calls from dense coastal scrub.

description: A slim brown-and-white cuckoo with a completely black bill and small white tail spots. Red orbital ring is often hidden. Less common than Yellow-billed Cuckoo on migration.
funFact: All-black bill separates it from Yellow-billed; small white tail spots from below.
fieldMarks: Small white tail spots (not large patches)
voice: Soft cooing and rapid cu-cu-cu when agitated.

description: A small nighthawk of open country, often seen hawking insects at dusk over fields and towns. Mottled gray-brown with a white wing bar; male has a white throat. Flight is buoyant and bat-like, usually low over the ground.
funFact: Feeds at dusk over open fields and roads; white wing bar flashes in low flight.

description: A widespread nighthawk with long pointed wings and a white wing patch. Mottled gray with a white throat on males. Often seen at dusk over towns and open woodland, sometimes perching on gravel rooftops.
funFact: Bold white wing patches visible in flight at dusk over villages and clearings.
voice: Nasal peent calls; booming display dive in breeding areas.

description: A ground-dwelling nightjar of open woodland and scrub. Intricately patterned in browns with a black chest collar and long tail. Sits on dirt roads at night, eyes reflecting red in headlights.
funFact: Freezes on forest roads at night; red eyeshine and whirring song at dusk.
voice: Whirring pauraque song at dusk and dawn.

description: A large nightjar with a big head and wide gape. Cryptically patterned in browns and grays; lacks the pauraque chest collar. Often heard calling from wooded edges on migration and in winter.
funFact: Named for its loud chuck-WILL-widow song from wooded edges at night.

description: A medium nightjar of dry scrub and open pine-oak areas. Warm brown with less contrast than Common Pauraque; male has white tail corners. Endemic to the Yucatan Peninsula including northern Belize.
funFact: Look for it in dry scrub of northern Belize; male shows white tail corners in flight.
voice: Rapid churring song from scrub at night.

description: A small dark swift with cigar-shaped body and stiff rapid wingbeats. Tail is short with projecting spines. Often seen in twittering flocks over towns and open country, especially on migration.
funFact: Cigar-shaped body and chittering flocks over towns; all-dark except pale throat.
voice: High chittering calls from feeding flocks.

description: A small hummingbird. Males have an iridescent ruby-red throat and emerald back; females are green above and whitish below. Common on migration and in winter in gardens and forest edge.
funFact: Male ruby gorget flashes in good light; common winter visitor to flowering gardens.
voice: Soft chipping calls; wings hum in flight.

description: A medium-sized hummingbird with bronzy-green upperparts and cinnamon underparts. Red bill with dark tip. Common in dry scrub, gardens, and open woodland throughout the region.
funFact: Cinnamon underparts and red bill make it one of the easier Amazilia hummingbirds to identify.
voice: Dry chipping and buzzy song from perches.

description: A medium hummingbird with bronzy-green upperparts, buff belly, and rufous tail corners. Red bill. Favors forest edge and flowering trees in the Yucatan region including Belize.
funFact: Rufous tail corners and buff belly distinguish it from Cinnamon Hummingbird.
voice: Chipping calls and squeaky song from canopy.

description: A large secretive rail of marshes and wet fields. Blackish with bold white spotting and a coral-red bill and legs. Rarely seen but sometimes flushes from dense wet grass.
funFact: Bold white spots on dark body and coral bill; flushes briefly from wet marsh edge.
voice: Grunting and whinnying calls from dense cover.

description: A plain dark rail of wet thickets and forest floor. Uniform dark brown with a greenish bill; very secretive. More often heard than seen, walking under dense cover near water.
funFact: Plain dark rail of wet thickets; listen for whistled calls at dawn.
voice: Whistled and grunting calls from dense cover.

description: A small rail of marshes and wet fields. Brown upperparts, gray face and breast, and black bib on breeding birds. Short yellow bill. Often detected by whinnying call in winter.
funFact: Gray face and whinnying call from marsh edges; black bib on breeding plumage.

description: A medium marsh bird with dark plumage, white flank stripe, and a red frontal shield on the bill. Swims and walks on floating vegetation. Yellow-tipped red bill and greenish legs.
funFact: Red shield on bill and white flank stripe on dark body; common on lily pads and marsh edge.
voice: Krek-krek calls and clucking from marshes.

description: A dark duck-like bird with a white bill and frontal shield. Lobed toes, not webbed feet. Swims on open water and grazes on shorelines; often in large flocks in winter.
funFact: White bill and dark body; lobed toes visible when climbing onto banks.
voice: Various clucks and croaks from flocks on water.

description: A spectacular marsh bird with purple-blue plumage, green back, red bill with yellow tip, and huge yellow feet. Climbs on floating vegetation in freshwater marshes and lily pads.
funFact: Iridescent purple-blue body and giant yellow feet on floating vegetation.
voice: Harsh cackling and grunting from marsh cover.

description: A small secretive waterbird of quiet forest streams and ponds. Brown with bold black-and-white striped neck and a striped face pattern. Male has pouches to carry chicks. Rarely seen, slipping into cover.
funFact: Striped neck and face on quiet forest ponds; slips into cover when disturbed.
voice: Soft whistling and clicking from streamside cover.

description: A large brown wading bird with white spots on the upperparts and a long decurved bill. Walks along marsh edges and streams hunting apple snails. Loud wailing calls carry at night.
funFact: Spotted brown body and screaming calls at night; hunts snails along marshy streams.
voice: Loud wailing and screaming calls, especially at night.

description: A slender black-and-white shorebird with extraordinarily long pink legs and a thin straight black bill. Forages in shallow water on mudflats and rice fields.
funFact: Impossibly long pink legs and black-and-white plumage on shallow mudflats.
fieldMarks: Black back and neck, white underparts

description: A elegant black-and-white shorebird with a unique upturned bill and long bluish legs. Breeding birds have cinnamon on the head and neck. Swings bill side to side while feeding in shallow water.
funFact: Upturned bill and graceful feeding motion in shallow lagoons.

description: A large chunky plover. Breeding adults are black below with white cap and black face; winter birds are gray-brown with a black armpit patch in flight. Runs and stops on mudflats and open shores.
funFact: Black armpit patch in flight; winter birds gray with bold black-and-white wings.

description: A medium plover with golden-spangled upperparts in breeding plumage; winter birds are gray-brown. Slenderer than Black-bellied Plover with no black armpit patch. Often on open fields and airfields.
funFact: Golden speckling on back in breeding plumage; gray-brown on open fields in winter.

description: A familiar plover of open ground with two black breast bands and a red eye ring. Runs on lawns, fields, and mudflats; performs broken-wing distraction display near nests.
funFact: Two black breast bands and loud kill-DEE calls from open fields and gravel areas.

description: A small dark plover with one black breast band and orange legs. Bill is orange with black tip. Common on mudflats and beaches in winter flocks.
funFact: Single breast band and orange legs on mudflats; smaller than Killdeer.

description: A small pale plover of sandy shores and river bars. Gray-brown above and white below with a narrow black collar across the breast. Pink legs and dark bill.
funFact: Narrow black collar and pale sandy appearance on river sandbars and beaches.

description: A long-necked sandpiper of open grasslands and airfields. Buffy with dark streaking above, large eye, and small head on a long neck. Flight shows cinnamon wing linings and fluttering style.
funFact: Long neck and fluttering song flight over open fields; cinnamon wing linings in flight.
voice: Wolf-whistle song in display flight over grasslands.
